strategy_summary: This week's research confirms three dominant signals for DealerWyze content: (1) the missed-lead math story is a credibility-through-specificity play that no competitor is owning on social -- exact dollar figures from industry data ($19,200/mo in wasted ad spend, $1M+/yr per store in missed service revenue) create scroll-stopping urgency; (2) 56% of dealer leads arrive after hours with no system to catch them, making the AI voice agent the single highest-conversion demo asset this channel has -- one real-time demo of a midnight lead getting caught and an appointment getting booked is worth ten feature lists; (3) founder authenticity is the content moat -- personal LinkedIn and TikTok accounts are outperforming brand pages by 3-5x in 2026, and a founder who was literally the dealer being burned by these problems has a story no corporate CRM vendor can replicate. Calendar this week balances three virality-first posts (cost math, chaos DITL, BHPH niche reveal) with three demo-conversion posts (AI midnight lead, stack comparison, stat-to-CTA) and one founder origin story that bridges both goals on LinkedIn.
top_viral_patterns: Cost Math Reveal: Build the math live on screen -- $45k in ads, 43% ignored, $19,200 wasted -- until the viewer can feel their own number. Specificity creates the scroll-stop. Shock then solution.
Chaos Day-in-the-Life: Show the dealer's actual chaotic Tuesday before DealerWyze. Multiple tabs, missed calls, sticky notes. Reality TV edit energy. Relatability drives shares.
Niche Pain Drop: Go hyper-specific to an underserved segment (BHPH, solo operator, lot with no BDC). Viewers in that niche feel seen and share instantly because nobody talks to them directly.
Founder Phone-Cam Authenticity: No polish, no studio, no script cards. Founder speaks directly into the camera about their own pain. Anti-corporate energy wins on both TikTok and LinkedIn in 2026.

Single Hard Stat as Hook: One number so specific it feels impossible to ignore. 78% of buyers go with the first dealer to respond. 47-minute average dealer response time. Leads in within 5 minutes are 21x more likely to convert. Build everything from that one number.
top_conversion_patterns: Live AI Demo (After Hours): Screen recording of the AI voice agent catching an 11pm lead, creating a task, and booking an appointment. Shows the product solving the #1 pain point with zero explanation required.

Stack Comparison with Dollar Amounts: Show the fragmented tool stack and its real monthly cost ($400 CRM + $200 SMS + $150 call tracker + $300 website = $1,230) against DealerWyze at $150. Math does the selling.
Founder DM Invitation: End LinkedIn posts with a soft 'DM me DEMO' CTA. Converts because the founder is offering themselves, not a sales funnel. Feels like access, not marketing.
Before/After Workflow Demo: Side-by-side screen recording showing the chaos workflow (4 tabs, 3 apps, missed notifications) versus the DealerWyze unified inbox. Visual contrast converts skeptics.

Niche Feature Reveal with Dashboard Clip: Show the BHPH module, the recall check, the AI scanner -- one specific feature per video, presented for a specific dealer type. Converts because it matches product to persona precisely.
oversaturated_formats_to_avoid: Generic AI hype videos with stock footage and voiceover -- viewers tune out immediately
Feature list explainers with screen recordings and no human presence
Corporate talking head interviews with multiple b-roll cuts
Comparison tables against Salesforce or HubSpot -- wrong ICP, wrong fight
Testimonial slideshows without a dealer face or real voice
fresh_opportunities: BHPH-specific content series -- this segment is massively underserved in dealer software content and has its own hashtag communities
Real-time after-hours lead catch demo -- no CRM vendor is showing this on social and it is the single most compelling demo asset available
Response time challenge content -- show the 47-minute industry average vs. DealerWyze instant response as a live race
Founder-to-founder LinkedIn content -- Tim talking directly to independent dealer owners as peers, not as a vendor
The hidden cost calculator reveal -- take a dealer's actual ad spend and reverse-engineer what they are losing through lead mishandling

days.title: The $19,200 You're Lighting on Fire Every Month
days.hook: You're spending $45,000 a month on ads. And you're ignoring 43% of your leads.
days.visual_opening: Tight shot of a cluttered dealership desk: coffee cup, calculator, printed lead sheet with red pen marks. Numbers build on screen in bold white text as the voiceover speaks.
days.script:
[0:00-0:03] SPOKEN: 'You're spending $45,000 a month on ads.' | TEXT ON SCREEN: '$45,000/mo in ads' (slams onto screen)
[0:03-0:06] SPOKEN: 'And you're ignoring 43% of your leads.' | TEXT ON SCREEN: '43% of leads → dropped'
[0:06-0:13] SPOKEN: 'That's not an opinion. That's industry data. The average independent dealer gets 150 internet leads a month. 43% never get a real call back.' | TEXT: '150 leads/mo | 43% dropped'
[0:13-0:19] SPOKEN: 'Let's do the math.' | TEXT BUILDS SLOWLY: '150 leads × 43% = 64 dropped | 64 × $300 ad cost = $19,200 wasted | 64 leads = ~8 deals = $42,400 missed gross'
[0:19-0:28] SPOKEN: '$19,200 in ad spend. Gone. Every month. Because the follow-up never happened.' | TEXT: '$19,200 burned every single month'
[0:28-0:37] SPOKEN: '78% of car buyers go with the first dealer to respond. The average dealer takes 47 minutes. By then, they've already called your competitor.' | TEXT: '78% buy from whoever calls first | Average response: 47 minutes'
[0:37-0:46] SPOKEN: 'If you're running your leads on spreadsheets, scattered texts, or a CRM that wasn't built for used car dealers -- this is your number. $19k a month. Every month.' | TEXT: 'Your missed leads = your competitor's sales'
[0:46-0:57] SPOKEN: 'DealerWyze catches every lead. Day and night. The AI voice agent answers after hours, creates a task, books appointments. That $19k? That's your growth budget.' | TEXT: 'DealerWyze | Every lead. Every hour.'
[0:57-1:00] SPOKEN: 'Free 14-day trial. No credit card. Link in bio.' | TEXT: 'Free trial → link in bio'
days.pacing_notes: Cut at every sentence. Text slams in hard on each stat. No background music until the math reveal at 0:13 where a low tension beat drops. Keep camera still -- let the words move. Speak slower than feels natural on the math section.
days.emotional_trigger: Financial loss aversion. The viewer does the math on their own lot in real time and feels the number personally.
days.caption: Industry data says 43% of dealer leads get dropped. At $300/lead in ad cost that's $19,200 a month. Do the math on your lot. Free trial in bio -- no credit card.
days.cta: Free 14-day trial, no credit card. Link in bio.
days.pattern_used: Cost Math Reveal -- build the arithmetic live on screen until the viewer calculates their own loss
days.pain_point_targeted: Missed leads and wasted ad spend from no follow-up system
days.business_goal: Top-of-funnel brand awareness among independent dealers scrolling TikTok. Drive free trial signups.
days.why_it_converts: Specific dollar amounts force a personal 'that's me' reaction. The CTA for a free trial arrives right after maximum financial pain is felt. No friction -- no credit card removes the last objection.
days.repurposing: LinkedIn carousel with each stat as a slide. Email campaign subject line 'The $19,200 you're losing every month.' Instagram Reel with text-only version. Sales deck opening slide.

days.title: A Tuesday Morning at a Lot With No System
days.hook: This is how most independent dealers start their Tuesday.
days.visual_opening: POV shot of a phone screen: 7 unread SMS threads from different numbers, 3 missed calls, 2 Facebook Messenger notifications, a CarGurus lead email buried in a Gmail inbox. Visible chaos, no opening spoken word for the first 2 seconds.
days.script:
[0:00-0:03] VISUAL ONLY: Chaotic phone screen -- missed calls, unread texts, lead notifications. No audio. Let the visual land. | TEXT OVERLAY builds slowly: 'Tuesday. 8:47am.'
[0:03-0:07] SPOKEN: 'This is how most independent dealers start their Tuesday.' | TEXT: 'No system. Just chaos.'
[0:07-0:15] Jump cut: Sticky notes across a monitor. An Excel spreadsheet open. Three browser tabs. SPOKEN: 'Inventory tracked in Excel. Leads coming in through texts. Customer history living in your head.' | TEXT: 'Inventory → Excel | Leads → text threads | Customers → memory'
[0:15-0:23] SPOKEN: 'A lead came in at 11pm last night. You had no idea. They already bought somewhere else this morning.' | TEXT: 'Overnight lead → missed → bought from your competitor'
[0:23-0:31] SPOKEN: '2pm. You try to follow up on last week's walk-ins. Half the contact info is in the wrong place. Some you can't find at all.' | TEXT: '23% of leads miss 24-hour follow-up'
[0:31-0:39] SPOKEN: '6pm. Another after-hours inquiry. Nobody answered. Your voicemail is full. 70% of people who hit voicemail will call a competitor within 30 minutes.' | TEXT: '70% call a competitor after hitting voicemail'
[0:39-0:49] SPOKEN: 'This is not a people problem. Your people are working hard. This is a systems problem. And it is costing you deals you never even knew you lost.' | TEXT: 'Not a people problem. A systems problem.'
[0:49-0:58] SPOKEN: 'DealerWyze is the operating system for independent dealers. One login. Every lead, every conversation, every vehicle, every customer. Your AI agent handles what happens when you go home.' | TEXT: 'DealerWyze | One login. Everything.'
[0:58-1:00] SPOKEN: 'Free trial. Link in bio.' | TEXT: 'Free trial → link in bio'
days.pacing_notes: Open with 2 seconds of silent visual -- this creates pattern interrupt on Reels. Each scene change is a quick cut. The emotional peak is at 0:39 (voicemail stat) -- hold a half-beat there before releasing into the solution. Keep total energy calm and matter-of-fact, not panicked. The chaos speaks for itself.
days.emotional_trigger: Recognition and relief. The viewer sees their own day on screen, feels understood, and is offered a systems reframe that removes personal blame.
days.caption: If this is your Tuesday morning, you're not alone. But you don't have to keep running it this way. The chaos isn't you -- it's the missing system. Free trial link in bio.
days.cta: Free trial in bio. Takes 5 minutes to set up.
days.pattern_used: Chaos Day-in-the-Life -- show the painful before in authentic detail before introducing the solution
days.pain_point_targeted: Operational chaos from disconnected tools and no unified lead management
days.business_goal: Shareability among dealer networks. This gets shared between dealer owners who recognize their own lot.
days.why_it_converts: Viewer sees their own day reflected without judgment. The 'not a people problem' reframe reduces shame and shifts blame to the missing system -- which DealerWyze provides. Reduces resistance to trying something new.
days.repurposing: TikTok with trending audio swapped in. YouTube Shorts. LinkedIn 'day in the life' text post. Email newsletter opener. Facebook group post for dealer communities.

days.title: I Built DealerWyze Because I Was the Dealer Getting Burned
days.hook: In 2022, I was running a used car lot and losing deals I didn't even know I was losing.
days.visual_opening: Founder speaking directly to camera, phone-held, no studio. Simple background -- could be the lot, an office, anywhere real. First frame is mid-sentence, no cold open countdown.
days.script:
POST TEXT (LinkedIn native format, personal account):

In 2022, I was running a used car lot and losing deals I didn't even know I was losing.

Not because of bad inventory.
Not because of bad salespeople.
Because my systems were a disaster.

Leads were coming in from Facebook, my website, CarGurus, phone calls -- and I had no single place to see all of them. I was paying:

$400/mo for a CRM not built for independent dealers
$200/mo for a separate texting tool
$150/mo for call tracking
$300/mo for a website builder
$180/mo to auto-post to social

$1,230 a month. Five tabs. None of them talking to each other. And I was still missing calls after hours. Still losing leads that came in at 11pm. Still following up three days late on deals that should have closed on day one.

I looked for something built for a dealer like me -- not a $1,500/mo enterprise CRM built for franchise groups. Something for a 1-10 rooftop independent lot running lean.

Nothing existed.

So I built it.

DealerWyze is what I wish I had in 2022. One login. Every lead, every conversation, every vehicle, every customer. AI that answers calls when you can't. A daily brief that tells you what actually matters that morning. Starting at $150 a month.

If you run an independent lot and you're still cobbling it together, I'd love to show you what we built.

Free trial link in the comments. No credit card required.

VIDEO (90 seconds, founder to camera): Mirror of the text post above, spoken conversationally. No teleprompter energy. Look into the lens. Pause after the cost list. Let the $1,230 number land before continuing.
days.pacing_notes: This is a reading-pace LinkedIn post, not a rapid-fire video. The power is in the specificity of the numbers and the 'nothing existed, so I built it' pivot. That line should be its own paragraph with white space above and below. End with a comment-based CTA to drive comment section engagement which feeds the LinkedIn algorithm.
days.emotional_trigger: Founder authenticity and peer recognition. LinkedIn dealer owners read this and see themselves in the 2022 version of Tim. The origin story creates trust that no marketing copy can manufacture.
days.caption: N/A -- LinkedIn post is the content. Put the free trial link in the first comment to keep the algorithm from suppressing an external link in the body.
days.cta: DM me DEMO or drop a comment and I'll send you the trial link directly.
days.pattern_used: Founder Origin Story -- built by a dealer for dealers, anti-corporate positioning, specific cost pain before the solution
days.pain_point_targeted: Tool fragmentation and subscription fatigue across 4-5 disconnected software products
days.business_goal: Inbound demo requests via LinkedIn DM. Founder credibility building for long-term brand trust.
days.why_it_converts: Founder credibility is the highest-trust conversion signal in B2B SaaS. Specifics (exact dollar amounts per tool) build believability. The 'nothing existed so I built it' arc is emotionally satisfying and hard to dismiss. LinkedIn inbound converts at 14.6% versus cold outreach. Personal account posts are getting 3-5x the reach of company page posts in 2026.
days.repurposing: Founder story TikTok monologue (60-second version). Instagram caption under a founder photo. Email newsletter 'our story' section. About page on dealerwyze.com. Sales call opener.

days.title: Watch the AI Catch a Lead at 11:47pm
days.hook: It's 11:47pm. A lead just came in. Nobody's at the lot.
days.visual_opening: Dark screen. Phone on a desk shows 11:47pm. Notification sound. The DealerWyze dashboard lights up with an inbound call indicator. AI voice agent icon appears active.
days.script:
[0:00-0:03] VISUAL: Dark room, phone screen shows 11:47pm, notification buzz. No spoken word for first second. | TEXT: '11:47pm'
[0:03-0:07] SPOKEN: 'It's 11:47pm. A lead just came in. Nobody's at the lot.' | TEXT: 'New inbound call → AI agent activates'
[0:07-0:15] SPOKEN: 'The DealerWyze AI voice agent picks up immediately. It asks what they are looking for. The caller says: 2020 Camry, budget around $15,000, needs financing, can come in tomorrow.' | TEXT: 'AI captures: Vehicle → Budget → Financing need → Availability'
[0:15-0:24] SPOKEN: 'The agent books a 10am appointment. Creates a follow-up task assigned to you. Tags the lead as hot. Sends a confirmation text to the customer.' | SCREEN RECORDING: DealerWyze task created, appointment on calendar, lead tagged 'hot', automated SMS confirmation sent. TEXT: 'Appointment booked | Task created | Lead tagged | SMS sent'
[0:24-0:33] SPOKEN: 'All of that happened before midnight. You are asleep. Your competitor is also asleep -- but their voicemail just picked up and lost that same type of lead.' | TEXT: 'You: sleeping | Competitor: voicemail'
[0:33-0:43] SPOKEN: '56% of dealer leads arrive after hours. Most lots have nothing to catch them. 78% of buyers go with the first dealer to respond. At 11:47pm that is no longer you -- unless your AI agent answers.' | TEXT: '56% of leads → after hours | 78% buy from whoever responds first'
[0:43-0:55] SPOKEN: 'You wake up at 7am. Confirmed appointment waiting in your calendar. Notes from the call already in the customer record. You walk in ready to close.' | TEXT: '7am → confirmed appointment waiting | No phone tag. No missed lead. No competitor.'
[0:55-1:00] SPOKEN: 'DealerWyze. Free 14-day trial. Link in bio.' | TEXT: 'Free trial → link in bio'
days.pacing_notes: The first 3 seconds are near-silent -- let the 11:47pm visual do the work. Screen recordings of the dashboard should be actual product footage, not mockups. The emotional beat at 0:24 ('you are asleep, competitor is getting voicemail') is the peak -- let it breathe for one full second before continuing. End clean and fast.
days.emotional_trigger: Competitive anxiety and the relief of having a solution running while you sleep. FOMO on after-hours leads converted to security through automation.
days.caption: Lead came in at 11:47pm. AI answered, booked a 10am appointment, and sent a confirmation text. No human. No missed lead. No competitor winning that one. Free trial in bio.
days.cta: Free 14-day trial. Link in bio. Your AI agent starts working tonight.
days.pattern_used: Live AI Demo -- real product, real use case, most compelling pain point, zero explanation required
days.pain_point_targeted: After-hours leads with no system to catch them (56% of all dealer leads)
days.business_goal: Direct free trial conversions and demo requests from dealers who recognize the after-hours gap in their current setup.
days.why_it_converts: Demonstrates the product solving the single highest-value pain point in the most visceral way possible. The viewer imagines their own 11pm lead getting caught. Screen recordings of real product create trust that mockups cannot. Ends with the benefit stated (appointment waiting in the morning) before the CTA -- the viewer is already sold before the ask.
days.repurposing: Website homepage hero video. Sales deck demo slide. Email GIF showing dashboard catching the lead. YouTube Shorts. LinkedIn demo post. Paid TikTok ad version with shortened hook.

days.title: The 4-Tab Dealer Stack vs. One Login
days.hook: Here's what most independent dealers are actually running right now.
days.visual_opening: Screen recording of a browser with 4 visible tabs: a generic CRM, a texting tool, Facebook Messenger, and a spreadsheet. Mouse cursor frantically clicking between tabs, losing track of where a lead notification came from.
days.script:
[0:00-0:05] VISUAL: Frantic tab switching across 4 different tools. No spoken word for 2 seconds -- let the visual chaos register. SPOKEN: 'Four tabs. Four logins. Four monthly bills. None of them talking to each other.' | TEXT: 'The average independent dealer's Monday morning'
[0:05-0:14] SPOKEN: 'Most independent dealers are paying $800 to $1,200 a month across tools that create more chaos than they fix.' | TEXT BUILDS ON SCREEN:
'CRM (not built for dealers): $400/mo
Two-way texting tool: $200/mo
Call tracking: $150/mo
Inventory website builder: $300/mo
Social auto-posting: $180/mo
Total: $1,230/mo'
[0:14-0:16] PAUSE. SPOKEN: 'Here is what that same operation looks like in DealerWyze.' | Hard cut to DealerWyze dashboard.
[0:16-0:28] SCREEN RECORDING DealerWyze: Unified inbox showing leads from Facebook, website, phone, CarGurus -- all in one feed. SMS thread open. AI task queue. Vehicle listings. Calendar with booked appointment. SPOKEN: 'One inbox for every lead source. Two-way SMS built in. AI voice agent answers calls. Auto-posting to Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and YouTube. Inventory website included. All of it.'
[0:28-0:36] SPOKEN: '$150 a month. One login. One bill. No tab switching.' | TEXT: 'DealerWyze Growth: $150/mo | Everything above included'
[0:36-0:46] SPOKEN: 'The question is not whether you can afford DealerWyze. It's how much longer you're going to pay $1,200 a month to stay confused.' | TEXT: '$1,230 to stay confused OR $150 to fix it'
[0:46-0:55] SPOKEN: 'Free 14-day trial. No credit card. You can set it up this afternoon.' | TEXT: 'Free trial → dealerwyze.com'
days.pacing_notes: The silent opening tab-switching moment must last at least 2 full seconds to register. The cost list needs to build slowly on screen -- do not show all items at once. The hard cut to DealerWyze dashboard should feel like a relief visually -- clean, organized, single-pane. The final rhetorical question at 0:36 should be read slower than the rest of the video.
days.emotional_trigger: Subscription fatigue and cognitive cost of tool fragmentation. The viewer calculates their own stack cost and feels the absurdity of the comparison.
days.caption: 4 tabs. 4 logins. $1,230/mo. vs. 1 login. $150/mo. That math should make you mad. Free trial at dealerwyze.com
days.cta: Free 14-day trial. dealerwyze.com. No credit card.
days.pattern_used: Before/After Stack Comparison with explicit cost math -- visual chaos versus visual clarity
days.pain_point_targeted: Paying $800-1,200/month across 4-5 disconnected tools with no integration
days.business_goal: Convert dealers already aware of the problem into trial signups. Price anchoring positions $150 as obvious.
days.why_it_converts: Price comparison is explicit and self-calculated by the viewer. The before/after visual contrast sells the product without requiring any explanation. The closing rhetorical question makes inaction feel irrational. YouTube Shorts viewers skew toward longer watch time tolerance -- this format fits the platform perfectly.
days.repurposing: TikTok version with faster pacing. Instagram Reel. LinkedIn slideshow post with cost breakdown as slides. Landing page A/B test hero video. Sales email attachment.

days.title: BHPH Dealers: What Your Payment Ledger Is Actually Costing You
days.hook: If you're doing buy-here pay-here and tracking payments in Excel, watch this.
days.visual_opening: Close-up of a printed Excel spreadsheet with handwritten corrections in red pen. Multiple columns, crossed-out numbers, hand-added notes in margins. Visually overwhelming.
days.script:
[0:00-0:04] VISUAL: Extreme close-up of a messy Excel printout with handwritten corrections. SPOKEN: 'If you're doing buy-here pay-here and tracking payments in Excel, watch this.' | TEXT: 'BHPH Dealers -- this one's for you'
[0:04-0:12] SPOKEN: 'BHPH is operationally harder than traditional retail. You're not just selling cars. You're running a loan portfolio. And most dealers are doing it with tools that were never built for it.' | TEXT: 'You're running a loan portfolio | You need loan portfolio tools'
[0:12-0:24] SPOKEN: 'Quick question. Without looking at a spreadsheet -- can you tell me: When did Account 047 make their last payment? Are they current? How many days past due? Who on your team is supposed to call them today? What is the remaining balance? What is the weekly interest accrual?' | TEXT: Building list of questions, each line appearing as spoken. No answers visible.
[0:24-0:33] SPOKEN: 'If any of those answers live in a spreadsheet, a notebook, or your head -- that is your portfolio risk. That is where delinquencies slip through.' | TEXT: 'Answers in Excel = portfolio risk'
[0:33-0:47] SPOKEN: 'DealerWyze has a built-in BHPH module. Payment ledger. Loan tracking. Delinquency flags. Account notes. Automated payment reminders to customers. Pulse surveys for check-ins. Everything in one place -- not five.' | SCREEN RECORDING: DealerWyze BHPH dashboard: loan account list, payment history timeline, delinquency flag highlighted, automated SMS reminder queued. TEXT: 'BHPH module built in | Payment ledger | Delinquency flags | Auto-reminders'
[0:47-0:57] SPOKEN: 'This is what running a BHPH portfolio that doesn't keep you up at night looks like. And it starts at $150 a month.' | TEXT: 'Sleep at night. Know your portfolio.'
[0:57-1:00] SPOKEN: 'Free trial, link in bio. BHPH dealers get a dedicated onboarding call.' | TEXT: 'BHPH onboarding included → link in bio'
days.pacing_notes: The series of rapid-fire questions at 0:12 should be delivered fast, almost overwhelming -- mirroring the feeling of trying to find that information in a spreadsheet. Then slow way down at 0:24 for the 'that is your portfolio risk' line. The dashboard reveal should be calm, organized, reassuring -- visual opposite of the opening spreadsheet.
days.emotional_trigger: Specific niche recognition ('this video is for me') followed by professional anxiety about portfolio blind spots, then relief through a concrete solution.
days.caption: BHPH dealers -- your payment tracking should not live in Excel. DealerWyze has a built-in BHPH module. Payment ledger, delinquency flags, auto-reminders, pulse surveys. Free trial + dedicated onboarding call. Link in bio.
days.cta: Free trial in bio. BHPH dealers get a separate onboarding session included.
days.pattern_used: Niche Pain Drop -- hyper-specific to the BHPH segment, asks questions that expose operational blind spots, then reveals the solution
days.pain_point_targeted: BHPH loan portfolio management on spreadsheets with no delinquency tracking, payment reminders, or unified ledger
days.business_goal: Penetrate the BHPH dealer segment which is underserved by generic CRMs and has specific operational pain that DealerWyze already solves.
days.why_it_converts: Hyper-specific ICP targeting makes BHPH dealers feel seen by a product for the first time. The rapid-fire question sequence creates controlled anxiety that the dashboard reveal releases. The dedicated onboarding offer reduces the friction of switching for a complex operational segment.
days.repurposing: TikTok version targeting BHPH hashtags. LinkedIn post for BHPH dealer communities and associations. Targeted Facebook ad to BHPH dealer interest segments. BHPH-specific landing page video. Direct outreach to BHPH United community members.

days.title: The One Stat That Changed How We Built DealerWyze
days.hook: 56% of dealer leads arrive after business hours. That one number changed everything for us.
days.visual_opening: Founder to camera, phone-held, conversational. Behind them: a window showing daylight -- convey that this is being filmed in the morning, matching the 'Sunday morning briefing' energy of LinkedIn audiences.
days.script:
POST TEXT (LinkedIn native, personal account):

56% of dealer leads arrive after business hours.

When I first saw that number, my first reaction was: we need to make sure DealerWyze catches all of them.

That's what the AI voice agent does. It answers calls at 2am. Qualifies the buyer. Creates a follow-up task. Books the appointment. Before you wake up.

But the second reaction hit harder.

That means the majority of a dealer's entire marketing budget is working while the dealer is sleeping. And most dealers have nothing in place to catch it.

Here's what I see in almost every conversation with an independent dealer:

- Spending $30,000 to $60,000 a month on ads
- 56% of the leads hitting after hours
- Nothing in place to catch them
- 78% of buyers going with whoever calls back first
- Average dealer response time: 47 minutes
- Leads contacted within 5 minutes convert at 21x the rate of leads contacted at 30 minutes

The math writes itself.

Dealers are not losing because of bad cars or bad pricing. They are losing because their systems can't keep up with the leads their ads are generating.

That is the gap DealerWyze was built to close.

If you run an independent lot and you're spending real money on leads while still missing after-hours calls -- DM me 'DEMO' and I will personally walk you through what we built.

No sales deck. No AE. Just me showing you how it works and whether it fits your operation.

VIDEO (2-3 minutes, founder to camera): Expand on the stat, tell the story of a specific dealer who was losing after-hours leads, show the before/after briefly. End with the DM offer.
days.pacing_notes: This is the highest-trust post of the week. LinkedIn algorithm rewards text-heavy personal posts that generate comments. The stat list in the middle should be formatted as a bulleted list with white space -- makes it scannable and shareable. The 'DM me DEMO' CTA is deliberately soft -- it signals access and peer conversation rather than sales. Post on Sunday morning for maximum weekday momentum.
days.emotional_trigger: Peer credibility and the fear of leaving money on the table. The stat list creates a 'checklist of my problems' moment. The personal DM offer feels like a favor, not a pitch.
days.caption: N/A -- LinkedIn post is the content. Reply to every comment within the first 2 hours to feed the algorithm.
days.cta: DM me 'DEMO' -- I'll personally walk you through it this week.
days.pattern_used: Single Hard Stat as Hook driving to founder authenticity and soft DM conversion CTA
days.pain_point_targeted: After-hours lead loss combined with slow response time -- the two most quantifiable causes of missed revenue
days.business_goal: Inbound demo requests via LinkedIn DM. Personal pipeline building for the founder. Brand positioning as the after-hours problem-solver.
days.why_it_converts: Opens with a credible industry stat (not a claim, a fact) which establishes authority before the product is mentioned. The DM CTA is the lowest-friction conversion path on LinkedIn -- feels like getting access to the founder directly. Personal account posts are getting 3-5x the organic reach of company pages in 2026 per research. Posting Sunday morning positions this for maximum engagement through Monday.
days.repurposing: TikTok stat-reveal format with the 56% number as the visual hook. Email campaign opener for warm leads. Twitter/X thread unpacking each stat. Sales email opening paragraph. Paid LinkedIn sponsored post.

top_3_strategies: Own the after-hours lead story: 56% of dealer leads arrive after hours and no competitor is running this as their primary content narrative. Every post that demonstrates the AI voice agent catching a lead after 11pm is a direct-response conversion machine. Make this the signature DealerWyze content asset. Post one after-hours demo per week minimum.

Use specific industry math to create urgency: The 43% lead drop rate, the $19,200 monthly waste, the $1M+ per store per year in missed service revenue -- these numbers from credible industry sources are more persuasive than any feature claim. Build every virality post around a number that forces the viewer to calculate their own loss. The math does the selling.

Founder-led LinkedIn as the primary trust and inbound pipeline channel: Personal account posts are outperforming brand pages by 3-5x in 2026. The founder who was literally the dealer being burned by these problems has a story zero competitors can replicate. Commit to 2-3 personal LinkedIn posts per week. DM-based conversion (not funnel links) converts at the highest rate for sub-$500/mo SaaS to SMB.
demo_conversion_formats: Live AI demo video: Screen recording of the AI voice agent catching an after-hours lead in real time. Show the appointment being booked, the task being created, the confirmation SMS going out. Most compelling single demo asset available.
Stack cost comparison: Before (4 tabs, $1,230/mo) versus after (DealerWyze, $150/mo). Visual contrast plus explicit math. Make the viewer do the subtraction themselves.
BHPH feature reveal: Dashboard walkthrough targeting BHPH dealers specifically. Loan ledger, delinquency flags, auto-reminders. Hyper-specific ICP targeting converts better than broad demos.

Founder DM invitation on LinkedIn: End every founder post with a soft 'DM me DEMO' offer. No funnel, no AE -- just the founder. Converts at significantly higher rates than link-in-bio CTAs for professional audiences.

Stat-to-solution walkthrough: Open with a single hard industry stat (56%, 47 minutes, 21x), explain what it means for the viewer's revenue, then walk through exactly how DealerWyze solves it. Education-to-product arc.
virality_formats: Cost math reveal: Build the arithmetic live on screen with text overlays. Every viewer does the math for their own lot. Specific dollar amounts outperform percentages for emotional impact.

Chaos day-in-the-life: Show the painful operational reality before DealerWyze. Multiple tabs, missed calls, sticky notes, spreadsheets. Reality TV edit energy. Dealers share this because it reflects their own morning.

Niche pain drop for BHPH: Content specifically for the buy-here pay-here segment gets shared within BHPH communities and forums because nobody makes content specifically for them. Niche precision beats broad appeal every time.
Founder origin story: 'Nothing existed for dealers like me, so I built it.' Anti-corporate energy with specific cost pain. Works on both TikTok and LinkedIn. Converts shares into DMs.

Single hard stat hook: One industry number delivered with deadpan confidence. 78% of buyers go with the first dealer to respond. The average dealer takes 47 minutes. No setup, no context -- just the number and then what it means.
mistakes_to_avoid: Polished corporate explainer videos with stock footage and professional voiceover -- kills authenticity and trust with the independent dealer audience
Feature laundry list content -- dealers do not buy features, they buy outcomes and time saved
Comparing DealerWyze to enterprise CRMs like Salesforce or VinSolutions -- wrong fight, wrong ICP, makes the product look like it is trying to play in a bigger league
Generic AI hype language ('AI-powered', 'leverage cutting-edge technology', 'transform your dealership') -- dealers are skeptical of AI marketing claims and respond to specific outcomes instead
Company page posting instead of founder personal account -- company page organic reach is down approximately 60% since 2024 on LinkedIn, personal profiles dominate content consumption
emotional_pain_points_this_week: Financial bleed from missed after-hours leads: 56% of leads hit after hours with no system to catch them. At a 10-15% close rate, that's $28,000-$40,000 in monthly gross profit sitting uncaptured.
Wasted advertising spend: Dealers spending $30,000-$60,000/mo in ads and mishandling 43% of the leads those ads generate. The math creates genuine anger and urgency.
Voicemail-to-competitor pipeline: 70% of callers who hit voicemail will call a competitor within 30 minutes. For dealers who have experienced this personally, this stat is visceral.

Subscription fatigue and tool chaos: The average SMB manages 30+ software subscriptions with 18% functional overlap. Independent dealers are running 4-5 tools that don't talk to each other and paying $800-1,200/mo for the privilege.

BHPH portfolio anxiety: Rising delinquency rates, tightening regulatory oversight, and no purpose-built software for loan portfolio management. BHPH dealers are running income-producing portfolios on spreadsheets and feeling the risk.
founder_storytelling_opportunities: The missed call that cost $8,000: A specific story about a deal lost because a call came in at 11pm and nobody answered. Name the vehicle, the buyer type, the gross. Make it concrete.
The month I realized spreadsheets were costing me more than a salesperson: The personal financial reckoning of doing the lead math on your own lot and seeing the number.
Why I walked out of my VinSolutions demo: The moment it became clear that enterprise CRMs were not built for a 10-car-lot independent dealer, and what happened next.
The BHPH dealer who called me crying: A real customer story (with permission) about a dealer who had accounts 90 days past due they didn't even know about because their tracking was broken.

What I built on a Sunday morning because nobody else was going to: The founding moment. Specific. Vulnerable. Human. This is the story that LinkedIn and TikTok algorithms amplify because it feels true.
strategic_summary.weekly_content_operating_system: Sunday: Produce the week's content calendar and brief. Monday-Tuesday: Film and post 2 virality-first pieces (TikTok and Instagram Reels). Wednesday: Publish the LinkedIn founder post (personal account). Thursday: Post the AI demo video (TikTok or YouTube Shorts). Friday: Post the stack comparison or conversion-focused piece. Saturday: BHPH or niche-specific content. Respond to every comment within 2 hours of posting -- the algorithm rewards comment velocity and the audience rewards personal engagement from the founder. Repurpose each video across platforms within 24 hours. Track: free trial starts, demo requests via DM, comment saves and shares.
strategic_summary.content_moat_strategy: DealerWyze's content moat is the founder who was the customer. No enterprise CRM vendor can post 'I was running the lot that was losing $19,000 a month in missed leads, so I built the tool that fixes it.' That story is owned permanently. The strategy is to make every piece of content trace back to that origin -- not as a one-time founding story, but as the lens through which every feature, every stat, every customer win is told. Tim does not post as a SaaS founder. He posts as a dealer who built a tool and is now showing other dealers what he learned. That positioning cannot be purchased, imitated, or outspent by VinSolutions, DealerSocket, or any other corporate vendor. It compounds with every post.
